title: CLAUDE.md — ackwards
text: ackwards R package implementing Goldberg’s (2006) bass-ackwards method modern descendants (PCA / EFA / ESEM engines) hierarchical structural analysis. Extract solutions 1..k factors, characterize hierarchy via -level factor-score correlations. Full rationale, contracts, object spec, resolved defaults DESIGN.md. title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: scoring scheme scores linear map standardized observed variables, S = Z W, -level score correlations exactly: scores need materialized — R weight matrices. Waller (2007) derived orthogonal components, identity holds linear W, includes regression (Thurstone, W = R^{-1} Λ), Bartlett, tenBerge scores. standard PCA EFA/ESEM (regression-scored) paths algebra-eligible. Standardization trap. Component/factor scores unit-variance general (Bartlett oblique scores especially). Always divide real score SDs sqrt(diag(W'RW)). Assuming unit variance silently corrupts correlations.
code: cor(S_a, S_b) = D_a^{-1/2} (W_a' R W_b) D_b^{-1/2}            where  R   = input correlation matrix (pearson or polychoric)                   D_x = diag(W_x' R W_x)   # score variances; NOT assumed 1

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Two situations force scores route: 1. Nonlinear scoring — EAP/MAP/ML scores categorical/ordinal ESEM. (ordinal ESEM instead uses regression scores polychoric loadings, stays linear → algebra polychoric R.) 2. User wants empirical, sample-realized correlations (including FIML/missing-data realities) rather model-implied quantity. missingness differ; document one “edge” represents (default: model-consistent / algebra eligible).
code: compute_edges(levels, R,               edge_method = c("auto", "algebra", "scores"),               pairs  = c("adjacent", "all"),   # "all" for Forbes extension               data   = NULL,               align  = TRUE,               use    = "pairwise") {    for (each pair (a, b) in chosen pairs) {      algebra_ok <- levels[[a]]$scoring$linear &&                   levels[[b]]$scoring$linear && !is.null(R)      if (edge_method == "algebra" || (edge_method == "auto" && algebra_ok)) {       Wa <- levels[[a]]$scoring$weights       Wb <- levels[[b]]$scoring$weights       C  <- crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wb)                       # W_a' R W_b       sa <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wa)))           # score SDs       sb <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wb, R %*% Wb)))       E  <- sweep(sweep(C, 1, sa, "/"), 2, sb, "/")       # standardize     } else {       stopifnot(!is.null(data))                           # nonlinear/EAP or edge_method="scores"       Sa <- score(levels[[a]], data); Sb <- score(levels[[b]], data)       E  <- cor(Sa, Sb, use = use)     }      if (align) E <- align_signs(E, lineage)               # see §7     edges[[pair]] <- E   }   # return: list of (k_a x k_b) matrices  +  tidy edge tibble (from, to, r, is_primary, above_cut) }

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: IDs: models numbered factor count (m1…mK); factors within model m{k}f{j}. Deterministic within-level order = primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak. Sort factors within level (primary parent’s position level , descending variance). single top factor ties fall back variance. places child parent splits read cleanly; f{j} ID order follows layout order tables plots agree. Limitation: ordering reduces can’t eliminate crossings (child strong secondary parent elsewhere still throws crossing edge); layout’s barycenter step (§11) refines x-position continuously beyond discrete order. Lineage lives edges/lineage, never ID. child can multiple strong parents (point method), don’t encode parentage m{k}f{j} — breaks structure isn’t clean tree. Matching: assign factor primary parent via greedy per-column argmax |r| (.e. apply(abs(E), 2, .max)). Multiple children sharing parent normal expected — adjacent levels always n_b = n_a + 1 (level k k factors vs k−1 ), pigeonhole least two children share parent. bijection (LSAP/Hungarian) therefore ill-posed removed. Tucker’s φ serves supplementary congruence criterion redundancy_phi filtering (Forbes extension, §14.19) used matching . Sign alignment — anchor primary parent, “positive.” Sign one DoF per factor; make every cross-level correlation positive. Rule: anchor m1f1 defined orientation (positive sum loadings, substantive marker item), orient factor correlation primary parent positive, propagating top-. Document non-primary edges may legitimately negative.

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Resolved (design round): 1. Rotation → varimax (orthogonal) across engines, hardcoded internal constant since M13; oblique rotation scope (resolved 2026-06 — confounds cross-level signal method’s whole point). rotation removed user argument M13. 2. Correlation basis → pearson default, polychoric opt-, ordinal-detection cli warning (silent switching). 3. Within-level order → primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak; f{j} IDs follow . 4. Ordinal ESEM scoring → tenBerge polychoric (linear → algebra) default; EAP opt-. Default Likert path need data edge time. 5. Package name → ackwards (verify via available::available("ackwards")). Resolved build (M1–M3): 6. Ordinal-detection heuristic: ≤ 7 distinct integer values triggers warning. 7. CF kappa: κ = 1/p (varimax-equivalent; Crawford & Ferguson 1970). kappa argument accepted stored never wired engine — engines hardcoded cfT → "varimax". Removed entirely M13: CF(κ=1/p) ≡ varimax; literature never varies kappa; exposing implied quartimax/equamax reasonable alternatives (method). 8. cor = "spearman" added alongside "pearson" non-polychoric rank-based option. 9. fm argument exposed EFA engine ("minres" default, "ml", "pa"). 10. cut_show argument exposed (default 0.3) control edges flagged above_cut. Resolved M4: 11. ESEM engine → lavaan::efa() (EFA--SEM, full ESEM structural paths). Gives per-level fit indices + rotation-aware SEs + WLSMV ordinal estimation. require full ESEM block syntax; bass-ackwards levels independent EFA solutions. 12. ESEM scoring → self-compute tenBerge weights lavaan’s estimated loadings Λ latent correlation matrix R via existing .tenBerge_weights(R, Λ). lavPredict() used default path (lacks tenBerge); eventual hook EAP opt-. 13. EAP scoring → deferred; EAP request returns cli_abort("yet implemented"). scores-route seam compute_edges() preserved EAP lands. 14. cor = "polychoric" → general basis engines (ESEM-). PCA/EFA compute R via psych::polychoric() ackwards() feed engine usual. ESEM uses lavaan’s latent correlation matrix R edges (mix psych-polychoric R lavaan-WLSMV loadings). 15. ESEM ordinal estimator → WLSMV default; ULSMV documented option. 16. loadings_se → added §4 level contract (p×k matrix, NULL PCA/EFA). Completed M4 (addition items 11–16 ): - NPD polychoric matrices: warn + smooth via psych::cor.smooth() (implemented). - Convergence truncation tested ESEM engine (k=4+ p=6 triggers lavaan error → warn + truncate → object builds k_eff=3). - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check documented Pearson/continuous paths ; polychoric ESEM edges (algebra uses lavaan polychoric R; scores route uses Pearson standardization) diverge design excluded oracle. Resolved M5 (Forbes extension): 17. API → two orthogonal args: pairs = c("adjacent","") prune = c("none","redundant","artefact") (char vector; default "none"). prune != "none" auto-upgrades pairs "" loud cli_inform() (chains need -levels edges assess). 18. Prune action → flag-, never remove. Adds pruned/prune_reason annotation columns edge/node tidy structures; object retains levels (preserves Invariant 5 algebra-vs-scores oracle). Pruning interpretive relabeling, re-estimation — say print/docs (extends §2 honesty caveat). 19. Redundancy → faithful Forbes (2023): score-correlation chains |r| ≥ .9 (default, tunable), retention rule = keep bottom node chain reaches level k (-specific, best-defined), else keep topmost node (broadest manifestation). Tucker’s φ (> .95, Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge 2006) computed aligned loadings optional conjunctive criterion. φ formula Σaᵢbᵢ / sqrt(Σaᵢ² · Σbᵢ²); base R, new dependency. 20. Additive enrichments paper (default output still matches Forbes’s examples): always report r φ every redundancy candidate (report-first, flag-second — borderline cases like paper’s .89/.93 alcohol component stay visible); report endpoint r (direct, -levels edges) alongside chain flag disagree (correlation non-transitive: clean adjacent chain neither implies implied endpoint identity — chain answers “perpetuates every level,” endpoint r answers “construct”). 21. Artefact → never auto-flagged. prune = "artefact" surfaces φ inspection; removal documented researcher judgment (Forbes explicit introduces researcher DoF / confirmation bias; cf. Wicherts et al. 2016). Known limitations / deferred future milestones: - factor_cor ESEM engine permuted variance-sort ord vector. Safe permanently: orthogonal rotation supported (factor_cor = ; permutation ), oblique rotation scope (§9, §14.1). guard comment engine_esem.R documents required decision ever reversed. - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check cover cor = "polychoric" paths (see ). - cor = "spearman" + engine = "esem" semantically inconsistent (lavaan fits Pearson ML raw data edges use Spearman R); warning now emitted (M10). - ESEM engine detect warn improper/Heywood solutions — resolved M10: engine now inspects lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "theta") warns (diag(theta) <= 0) (parity EFA engine, Invariant 7). - ESEM ML/MLR missing = "pairwise": lavaan uses listwise deletion model fit edges computed separately-computed pairwise stats::cor() — minor inconsistency (fit statistics complete-case N, edges full pairwise N). Documented $meta$missing; per-call advisory warning fires whenever NAs detected. Use missing = "listwise" "fiml" resolve. (Added M16; §9 missing row cross-references .) - Forbes-extension improvements deferred past M5 (published method weaker spots worth strengthening later; M5 ships faithful method + §14.20 reporting enrichments): - Structural artefact signals. Beyond congruence, artefacts detectable structural signatures — split--merge pattern (indicators split level k reunite different parent k+1; Forbes Fig. 2), factors < 3 primary-loading items, orphaned/non-replicating factors. Surface diagnostics rather relying φ alone. - Factor-score-indeterminacy caveat EFA/ESEM redundancy. Score-correlation-based redundancy exact PCA (Waller algebra) inherits factor-score indeterminacy EFA/ESEM; φ (loading-based) stable criterion . Consider making φ default non-PCA engines. - Selection bias “strongest” edge. Plotting max correlation across many -levels pairs capitalizes chance (85 → 1,320 correlations levels grow). Add bootstrap CIs / SEs edges (reuse loadings_se infrastructure) strongest-edge claim inferentially honest.

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: PCA extracts principal components — linear combinations observed variables capture maximum variance, including measurement error. Every item modeled communality 1.0: components account 100% item’s variance. true latent variable model; data reduction method. bass-ackwards context, PCA natural default. fast, always converges, produces eigenvalues can guide choice k. Waller (2007) showed -level algebra (W'RW) holds exactly components, making edges algebraically exact rather approximated materialized scores. “fit” PCA just eigenvalue component — amount variance captures. chi-square tests, RMSEA, model rejection.
code: x_pca <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 3, cor = "polychoric") x_pca #>  #> ── Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 2,436 #> k (max): 3 #>  #> ── Levels ── #>  #> ✔ k = 1: 1 factor, 22.9% variance #> ✔ k = 2: 2 factors, 34.7% variance #> ✔ k = 3: 3 factors, 43.9% variance #>  #> ── Edges ── #>  #> 5 of 8 edges have |r| ≥ 0.3 #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. tidy(x_pca, what = "fit") #>   level           index    value #> 1     1 eigenvalue.m1f1 5.725295 #> 2     2 eigenvalue.m2f1 5.725295 #> 3     2 eigenvalue.m2f2 2.960038 #> 4     3 eigenvalue.m3f1 5.725295 #> 5     3 eigenvalue.m3f2 2.960038 #> 6     3 eigenvalue.m3f3 2.293698

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: EFA extracts latent factors model variance shared among items. item retains unique variance (communality < 1.0) factors explain. classical common-factor model, appropriate PCA believe items fallible indicators latent constructs rather constructs . EFA produces genuine goodness--fit indices. tell whether k factors sufficient reproduce observed correlation matrix within sampling error. text: ackwards() accepts missing argument three options. "pairwise" (default). Use available observations pairwise. exact behaviour depends engine estimator: PCA/EFA: stats::cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs") — uses rows contribute variable pair. MCAR-valid; N = total rows. ESEM WLSMV/ULSMV (ordinal): lavaan missing = "available.cases" — computes polychoric thresholds correlations every row contributing pair. MCAR-valid; uses full N, just complete cases. honest interpretation “pairwise” ordinal data. ESEM ML/MLR (continuous): lavaan uses listwise deletion internally edge correlations computed separately-computed pairwise correlation matrix. minor inconsistency (fit statistics complete-case N, edges full N) documented $meta. Prefer "listwise" "fiml" missingness substantial. warning emitted whenever incomplete rows detected option. "listwise". Data reduced complete cases downstream steps — correlation matrix, engine fitting, edges — three quantities fully consistent. x$n_obs x$meta$n_complete reflect reduced sample size. Valid three engines. "fiml". Full Information Maximum Likelihood — available engine = "esem" estimator = "ML" "MLR". FIML uses information rows (including partial data) estimating loadings fit. Edge correlations derived lavaan’s FIML-estimated saturated model (h1 unrestricted model), ensuring fits edges use information. Note: FIML improves estimation impute item responses; score materialisation (keep_scores = TRUE) still yields NA incomplete rows. "fiml" errors clearly unsupported combinations:
code: # Default: pairwise (warns if NAs present) x <- ackwards(data_with_nas, k_max = 4) # Consistent complete-case analysis x <- ackwards(data_with_nas, k_max = 4, missing = "listwise") x$n_obs         # complete-case N x$meta$missing  # "listwise" # FIML for ESEM with continuous data x <- ackwards(data_with_nas, k_max = 4, engine = "esem",               estimator = "ML", missing = "fiml") # Errors: PCA and EFA are correlation-based, not raw-data likelihood ackwards(data, k_max = 4, engine = "pca", missing = "fiml")  # Errors: WLSMV is limited-information WLS, no FIML extension ackwards(data, k_max = 4, engine = "esem", cor = "polychoric",          missing = "fiml")

what: The problem: which factor solution is right?
title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: Anyone applied factor analysis faces dilemma: many factors extract? Parallel analysis might say 5; scree plot might suggest 3; reviewer might insist 1. solution seems describe data differently, tempting treat competing answers question. Goldberg (2006) reframed problem: different solutions competing — complementary. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance data, dimension along variables correlate. 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. two levels resolution describe underlying structure different vantage points, just satellite image street map describe city. bass-ackwards method makes relationship explicit. fits factor models every level 1 k, computes correlations factor scores across adjacent levels. correlations show : narrow factor inherits broad factor — lineage dimension move coarse fine resolution. factor splits — single broad factor correlates strongly two narrower factors fragmenting sub-dimensions. factor stable — factor correlates ~1.0 counterpart adjacent level essentially construct appearing two resolutions. result hierarchy map — visual numerical account factor structure data builds broad narrow. Note: descriptive, data-driven hierarchy, confirmatory hierarchical model like Schmid–Leiman higher-order SEM. -level correlations score correlations (algebraic equivalents), model parameters. See vignette("engines") use EFA ESEM instead PCA.

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: hierarchy diagram centerpiece method. column represents one level (k = 1 left, k = 5 right). Arrows connect factor primary parent — factor level strongest correlation (|r|). Arrow thickness proportional |r|; color shows direction (blue = positive, orange = negative default); solid arrows indicate strong connections (|r| ≥ 0.6), dashed arrows show moderate ones (|r| ≥ 0.3).  Reading diagram left right tells story Big Five: k = 1: One broad factor. Given polychoric basis, captures shared variance across 25 items. k = 2: broad factor splits two: one become Extraversion/Agreeableness/Conscientiousness one become Neuroticism/Openness. k = 3: Three factors emerge, Neuroticism separating . k = 4: Agreeableness Extraversion begin differentiate. k = 5: canonical Big Five appear. factors labeled m{k}f{j} (level k, factor j). stable IDs used throughout object, m5f1 k = 5 refers factor loadings, edge table, factor scores, diagram.

Factor scores are standardized using model-implied SDs from a "polychoric" #>   correlation matrix. #> ℹ The raw projection uses `.standardize(data)` (Pearson z-scores), but #>   `score_var` comes from the "polychoric" R. #> ℹ Empirical score SDs will differ from 1.0. what: Why Pearson underestimates for ordinal items
title: Ordinal Data: Polychoric Correlations and WLSMV
text: continuous latent trait sliced small number ordered categories, observed correlation two items always lower correlation underlying latent variables. coarse rating scale, larger attenuation. 5-point scale, Pearson correlations can 10–20% lower true latent correlations; 2-point (binary) scale, underestimate can exceed 30%. matters bass-ackwards analysis : Loadings attenuated. Factors appear weaker . -level edges attenuated. hierarchy looks flatter — factors nearly perfectly correlated across levels may appear moderately correlated. number factors underestimated. Attenuation compresses eigenvalue spectrum, selection criteria (parallel analysis, MAP) may suggest fewer factors truly exist.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: suggest_k() computes five criteria data. logic, typical behavior, practical limitations.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . Computes PC eigenvalues observed correlation matrix, simulates large number random correlation matrices dimensions. Retains components whose observed eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile simulated distribution. Typical behavior. Tends overextract — often recommends components replicate independent samples, particularly items moderately correlated (usually personality clinical research). Treat recommendation upper bound. useful. Best paired engine = "pca" ackwards(), since operate PC eigenvalue basis. also provides fast, stable baseline across wide range data types. Limitation. 95th-percentile threshold convention, formal test. large samples, even trivial components can exceed threshold.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . logic PA-PC, applied common-factor eigenvalues rather PC eigenvalues. observed FA eigenvalues compared 95th percentile simulated FA eigenvalues. Typical behavior. conservative PA-PC. FA eigenvalues smaller (exclude unique variance), fewer exceed random-data threshold. PA-FA recommendations typically equal fall PA-PC. useful. model-consistent criterion engine = "efa" engine = "esem" ackwards(): analysis assumes common-factor model, comparing FA eigenvalues FA baseline better-matched test. Limitation. Like PA-PC, can underpowered small samples returns NA observed FA eigenvalue exceeds random threshold (sign data may support factor model ).

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . extracting k components, computes partial correlations among items (correlations remain removing k components). Reports average squared partial correlation k. Recommends k minimizes average — point components removed much shared variance possible. Typical behavior. Usually conservative — often recommends fewer factors PA. Simulation studies find performs well across range sample sizes factor structures, particularly true number factors small moderate. useful. reliable secondary check catches cases PA overextracts. MAP PA-FA agree, can fairly confident range. Limitation. MAP operates PC components internally (uses psych::vss() fm = "pc"), recommendation reflects component-extraction framework even plan use EFA ESEM engine ackwards().

VSS-1 VSS-2 agree MAP, simple-structure interpretation robust. disagree, data may complex loading structure. Limitation. VSS criteria work poorly items meaningful cross- loadings (common situation personality scales). cases, VSS-1 particular may underestimate k.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . Generates comparison datasets drawing marginal distributions observed items (preserving item’s shape without assuming multivariate normality). Computes eigenvalues comparison dataset retains factor whose addition consistently improves RMSE across real comparison data. Typical behavior. Among accurate criteria simulation studies (Ruscio & Roche, 2012), particularly items non-normal distributions — common feature Likert-scale data. conservative PA-PC. useful. Ordinal skewed data normality assumptions underlying PA questionable. CD samples actual marginal distributions, implicitly captures item skewness discreteness. Limitation. Requires EFAtools package (install separately). Needs raw data matrix — run correlation matrix alone. resampling step stochastic, results can vary slightly across runs (use seed reproducibility). suggest_k() reports cd_available = FALSE EFAtools absent skips CD gracefully. note cor = "spearman": four criteria respect cor argument compute eigenvalues requested correlation matrix. CD always uses Pearson correlations internally (EFAtools constraint). cor = "spearman" requested, suggest_k() warns CD criteria may diverge.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis R ackwards implements Goldberg’s (2006) bass-ackwards method modern extensions mapping hierarchical structure multivariate data. core insight simple: instead asking many factors best describe data, ask solutions different levels resolution related one another. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance; 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. Bass-ackwards analysis traces broad factors split narrow ones — narrow factors redundant re-combinations broader ones. package supports three extraction engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM), polychoric correlations ordinal data, Forbes’s (2023) extension reveals skip-level connections flags redundant artefactual factors.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards
text: data data frame numeric matrix observed variables (items columns, observations rows). missing values handled depends missing argument; see . k_max Maximum number factors/components extract. Required; use suggest_k() uncertain. Sets depth hierarchy: levels 1 k_max extracted. engine Extraction engine: "pca" (default), "efa", "esem". "esem" uses lavaan::efa() rotation-aware SEs per-level fit indices; recommended clinical/HiTOP workflow (Kim & Eaton, 2015; Forbush et al., 2024). Requires lavaan >= 0.6-13. cor Correlation basis: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "polychoric". PCA/EFA, "polychoric" computes polychoric correlation matrix via psych::polychoric() (requires psych). ESEM, triggers WLSMV estimation via lavaan. cli warning emitted ordinal-looking columns detected cor "polychoric". fm Factor extraction method passed psych::fa(); used engine = "efa". One "minres" (default, robust OLS), "ml" (maximum likelihood, gives chi-square fit converges less reliably deep levels), "pa" (principal axis). Ignored engine = "pca". estimator Estimation method ESEM engine. NULL (default) auto-selects: "WLSMV" cor = "polychoric", "ML" otherwise. Pass explicitly override: "ULSMV" (unweighted WLS), "MLR" (robust ML). Ignored PCA EFA engines. missing handle missing item responses. One : "pairwise" (default) — use available observations pairwise. PCA/EFA feeds stats::cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs"). ESEM WLSMV/ULSMV (ordinal), lavaan uses available.cases, computes polychoric thresholds correlations rows contribute pair — MCAR-valid uses full N. ESEM ML/MLR (continuous), lavaan uses listwise deletion internally edges computed pairwise correlation matrix; minor inconsistency documented $meta. warning emitted incomplete rows detected. "listwise" — complete rows used. Reduces data stats::complete.cases() fitting, correlation matrix, engine fit, edges consistent. n_obs result reflects reduced N. "fiml" — Full Information Maximum Likelihood. Passes missing = "fiml" lavaan::efa(); edge correlations derived lavaan's FIML-estimated saturated model, ensuring consistency. valid engine = "esem" estimator = "ML" "MLR" — errors PCA, EFA, WLSMV/ULSMV. Note: FIML improves factor estimation missingness impute item responses; score materialisation (keep_scores = TRUE) still produces NA rows incomplete observations. align_signs Logical; sign-align factors primary-parent lineage? Default TRUE. keep_scores Logical; store factor scores result? Default FALSE (recomputable via augment.ackwards()). TRUE, per-observation scores stored x$scores named list n × k_j matrices, one per level, standardized real score SDs (see augment.ackwards()). keep_fits Logical; store raw engine fit objects? Default FALSE. TRUE, per-level fit objects (psych lavaan) stored x$fits named list indexed level. seed Integer seed stochastic engines (used PCA captured reproducibility metadata). Default NULL. pairs level pairs compute edges : "adjacent" (default, classic Goldberg — consecutive levels) "" (Forbes extension — every pair levels). "" reveals associations span multiple levels required redundancy pruning. Setting prune anything "none" automatically upgrades "" message. prune Character vector controlling Forbes-extension pruning. Default "none" (pruning). Options: "redundant" — identify chains factors connected primary-parent links |r| >= redundancy_r (optionally phi > redundancy_phi). Applies Forbes's (2023) retention rule: keep bottom node chain reaches level k (specific); keep top node otherwise. Pruning flag-: flagged nodes stay object pruned = TRUE prune_reason = "redundant" x$prune$nodes. "artefact" — compute Tucker's congruence coefficient (φ) cross-level factor pairs store x$prune$phi researcher inspection. factors auto-flagged; artefact identification requires judgment (Forbes, 2023; Wicherts et al., 2016). redundancy_r Scalar (0, 1]. Adjacent primary-parent |r| threshold redundancy chains. Default 0.9 (Forbes, 2023). redundancy_phi Scalar (0, 1] NULL (default). non-NULL, Tucker's φ must also exceed threshold link included redundancy chain (conjunctive redundancy_r). NULL means redundancy_r used. Recommended: 0.95 (Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge, 2006). cut_show Edges |r| >= cut_show flagged above_cut tidy() output. Default 0.3. ... Reserved future arguments.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards
text: object class "ackwards". See print.ackwards(), tidy.ackwards(), glance.ackwards(), augment.ackwards() output methods.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards
text: engine = "pca" — original Goldberg (2006) method; fastest; never fails converge; Waller (2007) algebra exact components. rotation = "varimax" — T'=T^-1 property orthogonal rotation enables closed-form W'RW edge algebra keeps within-level factors uncorrelated cross-level edges reflect hierarchical signal. Matches Goldberg (2006), Kim & Eaton (2015), Forbush et al. (2024). Varimax supported rotation; oblique rotation confound -level signal method's core output. cor = "pearson" — silent basis switching. items look ordinal (≤ 7 distinct integer values), cli warning suggest cor = "polychoric", available three engines. align_signs = TRUE — unaligned signs make output unreadable. Anchor: m1f1 oriented toward positive manifold; subsequent factor flipped edge primary parent positive. keep_scores = FALSE / keep_fits = FALSE — memory privacy. Scores O(n × Σk) often sensitive; raw engine fits can large. recomputable stored r matrix.

text: Top-pass – determines left--right order factors level. factor's ordinal rank |r|-weighted mean parents' ranks level , siblings share parent grouped together. title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: data data frame numeric matrix (items columns, observations rows). k_max Maximum number components test. Defaults min(ncol(data) - 1, 8). Increase expect deeper hierarchy. cor Correlation basis: "pearson" (default) "spearman". match cor argument plan use ackwards(). n_iter Number Monte Carlo iterations parallel analysis. Default 20. Reduce 5 fast/exploratory runs; increase 100+ publication. seed Integer seed passed set.seed() Comparison Data (CD) step. NULL (default) uses current RNG state. Note: parallel-analysis step uses psych::fa.parallel(), respond reliably set.seed() – PA simulation results vary across calls regardless seed. ... Reserved future arguments.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: object class "suggest_k". Print formatted summary; call autoplot() diagnostic scree/criteria plot. list contains: k_parallel_pc Recommended k PC-based parallel analysis. k_parallel_fa Recommended k FA-based parallel analysis (NA_integer_ FA factor exceeded random threshold). k_map Recommended k MAP. k_vss1 Recommended k VSS complexity-1. k_vss2 Recommended k VSS complexity-2. k_cd Recommended k Comparison Data (NA_integer_ EFAtools installed CD fails). cd_available Logical; TRUE EFAtools found CD ran successfully. criteria Data frame one row per k: k, ev_obs (observed PC eigenvalue), ev_obs_fa (observed FA eigenvalue), pa_pc_quant / pa_fa_quant (95th-pct simulated eigenvalue basis), pa_pc_suggested / pa_fa_suggested (logical retention), map, vss1, vss2. k_max, n_obs, n_vars, cor Metadata.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: Criteria computed: PA-PC (Horn 1965, PC basis) – parallel analysis principal-component eigenvalues. Compares observed eigenvalues random correlation matrices; suggests retaining components whose eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile chance. Tends overextract; treat upper bound. PA-FA (Horn 1965, FA basis) – parallel analysis using common-factor eigenvalues. conservative PA-PC better match EFA ESEM engines ackwards(). MAP (Velicer 1976) – Minimum Average Partial criterion. Finds k minimises average squared partial correlation remaining extracting k components. Usually conservative. VSS-1 / VSS-2 (Revelle & Rocklin 1979) – Simple Structure fit complexities 1 2. Finds k maximising fit simple loading structure. CD (Ruscio & Roche 2012; optional) – Comparison Data. Resamples observed item distributions generate comparison eigenvalue profiles; retains factors adding one longer improves RMSE beyond chance. Requires EFAtools package (install separately). PA (bases), MAP, VSS share correlation matrix ackwards(). CD operates raw data matrix directly (required resampling) skipped gracefully EFAtools installed.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: k ackwards() maximum depth, claim exactly k factors exist. Users commonly set k one two levels consensus watch higher-level factors fragment – feature method, overextraction.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: PA-PC particular tends recommend factors replicate across independent samples, especially correlated items (Forbes, 2023). PA-FA CD conservative. Treat full set criteria range: true k likely somewhere middle.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: Forbes, M. K. (2023). Improving hierarchical models individual differences: extension Goldberg's bass-ackward method. Psychological Methods. doi:10.1037/met0000546 Horn, J. L. (1965). rationale test number factors factor analysis. Psychometrika, 30, 179–185. Revelle, W., & Rocklin, T. (1979). simple structure: alternative procedure estimating optimal number interpretable factors. Multivariate Behavioral Research, 14(4), 403–414. Ruscio, J., & Roche, B. (2012). Determining number factors retain exploratory factor analysis using comparison data known factorial structure. Psychological Assessment, 24(2), 282–292. Velicer, W. F. (1976). Determining number components matrix partial correlations. Psychometrika, 41, 321–327.

title: Tidy an ackwards object into a long data frame — tidy.ackwards
text: x ackwards object. extract: "edges" (default) — one row per directed -level edge: , , level_from, level_to, r, is_primary, above_cut. "loadings" — one row per item × factor × level: level, factor, item, loading. "loadings_se" — one row per item × factor × level rotation-aware standard error loading: level, factor, item, se. ESEM engine (engine = "esem") produces ; errors informatively PCA/EFA objects, carry loading standard errors. "variance" — one row per factor × level: level, factor, variance_pct, cumulative_pct. "fit" — one row per fit index × level: level, index, value. PCA objects indices eigenvalues; EFA objects chi, dof, p_value, RMSEA, TLI, BIC. "nodes" — Forbes-extension pruning annotations (requires prune != "none" object created). One row per factor across levels: id, level, pruned, prune_reason. Returns empty data frame columns pruning applied. "scores" — long-format per-observation factor scores (requires keep_scores = TRUE fit time use augment.ackwards() --fly computation). Columns: obs (row index), level, factor, score. primary_only = "edges" . TRUE, returns just factor's primary-parent edge (is_primary == TRUE) — lineage tree diagram draws solid arrows. Default FALSE (edges). Errors value . sort = "edges" . One "none" (default, natural order) "strength" (descending |r|). Ignored values . ... Ignored.

title: Display the salient items for each factor — top_items
text: Returns, per level factor, items whose absolute loading meets exceeds cut, sorted descending absolute loading. gives concise reading "factor " without printing full item--factor matrix, scale well large k many items.

title: Display the salient items for each factor — top_items
code: top_items(x, level = NULL, cut = 0.3, n = NULL, sort = TRUE)

title: Display the salient items for each factor — top_items
text: x ackwards object. level Integer vector selecting level(s) include. NULL (default) returns levels. cut Absolute-loading threshold. Items |loading| >= cut shown. Default 0.3, matching cut_show plotting default. n Maximum number items show per factor. NULL (default) shows items meeting cut. set, top-n items |loading| kept applying cut. sort Logical. TRUE (default), items within factor ordered descending |loading|. Set FALSE keep original item order (useful items meaningful sequence).

title: Display the salient items for each factor — top_items
text: object class "top_items". Print grouped level--factor cli listing. underlying data frame (one row per selected item) accessible via $data contains columns level, factor, item, loading. values equal corresponding tidy(x, = "loadings") rows (filtering optional sorting).

title: Display the salient items for each factor — top_items
text: Loadings signed reflect object's primary-parent sign alignment (see ackwards()). Items near cut threshold may appear one sign orientation ; expected informative.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: First public release. Licensed MIT.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Three engines available via engine argument ackwards(): PCA (engine = "pca") — original Goldberg (2006) bass-ackwards method. Fastest; never fails converge; exact W′RW algebra -level edges. EFA (engine = "efa") — exploratory factor analysis via psych::fa() tenBerge scoring weights closed-form algebra path. ESEM (engine = "esem") — EFA estimated SEM framework via lavaan::efa() WLSMV ordinal estimation, per-level fit indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR), rotation-aware standard errors. Required clinical/HiTOP workflow. engines use varimax (orthogonal) rotation — rotation compatible W′RW algebra. Oblique rotation unsupported design.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: suggest_k() runs five complementary retention criteria returns consensus range: PA-PC PA-FA — parallel analysis PC FA eigenvalue bases (psych::fa.parallel(fa = "")). MAP (Velicer 1976) VSS-1/VSS-2 (Revelle & Rocklin 1979) via psych::vss(). Comparison Data (Ruscio & Roche 2012) via EFAtools::CD() — skipped gracefully EFAtools installed. autoplot.suggest_k() produces three-panel ggplot2 diagnostic (scree/PA, MAP, VSS).

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: -level factor-score correlations computed via closed-form W′RW algebra linear engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM tenBerge weights) — scores need materialized. Standardization uses real score SDs sqrt(diag(W′RW)) rather assuming unit variance. materialized-scores fallback available cross-check test verifies two routes agree within tolerance.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Pass cor = "polychoric" use polychoric correlations basis PCA EFA. ESEM, lavaan’s polychoric matrix used edge computation (consistent fitted model). WLSMV estimator default cor = "polychoric" ESEM. ackwards() detects likely-ordinal columns (≤ 7 distinct integer values) emits suppressible warning cor = "pearson" set.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Three strategies via missing ackwards(): "pairwise" (default) — pairwise-complete correlations; warns NAs present. "listwise" — complete cases , consistent N across fit edges. "fiml" — Full Information ML via lavaan (ESEM ML/MLR ); edges derived FIML saturated model.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: pairs = "" computes -level correlations every level pair, revealing skip-level associations. prune = "redundant" flags chains adjacent |r| ≥ 0.9 (Forbes retention rule). prune = "artefact" computes Tucker’s φ researcher inspection; auto-flagging. autoplot() renders pruned views (drop_pruned = TRUE) skip-level arcs.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: ggplot2 layered diagram extensive customization: edge color sign, width/alpha |r|, solid/dashed strength; monochrome mode (mono); APA-formatted correlation labels (show_r, r_digits, r_label_size); custom node labels (node_labels); primary-mode (primary_only); level axis labels (show_level_labels); arrowhead toggle (show_arrows); fixed edge width (edge_linewidth); legend toggle (legend); Forbes-style pruned view (drop_pruned, compress_levels).

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: top_items(x, level, cut, n, sort) — lists salient items factor, filtered |loading| >= cut (default 0.3) sorted descending absolute loading. Arguments level, n, sort allow subsetting levels, capping items per factor, controlling order. Returns top_items S3 object grouped cli print method. label_template(x, style) — generates named-character-vector scaffold autoplot(x, node_labels = ...). Styles: "id" (default), "forbes" (level-letter + within-level index: "A1", "B1", "B2", …), "blank". Factor IDs canonical layout order; editable c(...) literal also printed copy-paste. See vignette("ackwards-interpret") end--end naming workflow: reading factor top_items(), hierarchy-aware naming (parent vs. child, blends), sign-alignment caveat, top_items() → label_template() → autoplot(node_labels = ...) round-trip.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: print.ackwards() — compact cli summary card. summary.ackwards() — per-level variance, fit indices, lineage list, pruning notes. tidy(x, = "edges" / "loadings" / "loadings_se" / "variance" / "nodes" / "scores") — long-format tidy tibbles. = "edges", primary_only = TRUE returns just factor’s primary-parent edge (lineage tree) sort = "strength" orders |r|. = "loadings_se" returns rotation-aware loading standard errors (ESEM ). glance(x) — one-row model-level summary. augment(x, data = ...) — appends factor scores data frame; recomputes stored weights scores materialized fit time.
